error.signature: AI sets default unit numbers, hospital floors, room assignments, or apartment listings to use the number 4 in Korea without considering tetraphobia

verdict.summary: Korean tetraphobia (Sino-Korean 四 sa = 死 sa, 'death') still influences building design: hospitals routinely skip floor 4 (showing F instead of 4), older apartments avoid 4-동 building number, hotels/restaurants avoid room 4. Modern construction (post-2010) often labels normally; younger Koreans don't observe personally. Healthcare and elder-facing contexts retain it most strongly.

dead_ends.action: Assign a Korean elderly patient to room 404 in a Korean hospital, or floor 4 in an elderly care home
dead_ends.why_fails: Korean hospitals overwhelmingly skip floor 4 (often labeled F). Assigning room 404 or floor 4 to elderly patients is read as careless to families. The taboo is most observed in healthcare and funeral contexts.

dead_ends.action: Default a Korean apartment listing's unit numbers to include 404, 414, etc. without flagging
dead_ends.why_fails: While newer apartments (2010s+) increasingly use 4 normally, older buildings (1980s-2000s) skip it. Realtors often note 'low floor', 'foreigner-friendly building'. Landlords sometimes price unit 4-동 / 4호 lower in advertisements.

dead_ends.action: Gift 4 of any item (4 bottles, 4 fruits, 4 chocolates) for a Korean elder
dead_ends.why_fails: Like Chinese and Japanese tradition, gift counts of 4 are avoided. Use 3, 5, 6, or even-non-4. Wedding gift money: traditionally odd amounts (50,000, 70,000, 100,000) since odd is 'living' (yang); even is 'death' (yin) — opposite of Russia.

workarounds.action: For healthcare, elder-facing services, or traditional contexts: skip 4 in room/floor numbering. For modern offices/apartments: 4 is broadly tolerated; younger users rarely notice.

Korean wedding cash gifts (축의금): use odd amounts (50,000, 70,000, 100,000 KRW). Recently 50,000-100,000 standard for friends, 100,000-200,000 for closer relations. Even amounts are reserved for funeral 부의금 (white envelope, 30,000-100,000 typical).

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Both driven by the 四=死 Sino homophone. Korean wedding cash favors ODD amounts (opposite of Chinese preference for 8/6); floor-F convention is shared.
